target.name: Monarch Tractor
target.founder: Praveen Penmetsa, Mark Schwager, Carlo Mondavi, Zachary Omohundro

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $240M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Percepto
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

private.name: Tekever
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Monarch Tractor is a Series C autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $240M+ to date. The peer universe consists of 6 private companies in the same sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, and Archer Aviation; no direct public comparables exist in this frontier-tech domain.

Stage-progression analysis, the applicable triangulation method here, uses the $240M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C to subsequent-stage progression, yielding $408M. Peer-median comparison was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers—while four sector peers exist, the narrow filters required for meaningful comparison did not yield the minimum three datapoints needed to support robust peer-based valuation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $347M to $469M, with a median point estimate of $408M. The single-method reliance introduces material uncertainty; a peer-median benchmark, if sufficient filtered comparables become available, would meaningfully improve conviction. Additionally, stage-progression assumes typical venture step-ups, which may not hold if Monarch's market position, customer traction, or capital efficiency diverges materially from median Series C cohorts. Cash-on-hand, burn rate, and near-term revenue milestones would anchor this range more precisely.
